annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

tools.description: Close a GitHub issue with a closing comment. Use this when work is complete, the issue is no longer relevant, or it's a duplicate. The closing comment should explain the resolution or reason for closing. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be closed. Target: *.

body.description: Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to close. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

state_reason.description: The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.

tools.name: close_issue

tools.description: Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *. Supports reply_to_id for discussion threading.

body.description: The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.

reply_to_id.description: Node ID of the discussion comment to reply to, enabling threaded discussion comments. When provided, the new comment is posted as a reply to the specified top-level discussion comment. If the given node ID belongs to a nested reply, the handler automatically resolves it to the top-level parent. Only applicable for discussion comments — ignored for issue and pull request comments.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.

tools.name: add_comment

tools.description: Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: add_labels

tools.description: Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to remove labels from. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: remove_labels

tools.description: Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.

assignee.description: Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.

assignees.description: GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: assign_to_user

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

tools.description: Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.

details.description: Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).

reason.description: A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.

tools.name: report_incomplete

tools.description: Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization

message.description: The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols

owners.description: Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')

tools.name: mention_owners

content.text: {"number":58427,"title":"[ContentUnderstanding] Docs: Document AnalyzeUsageDetails and GetUsage() in README","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding` **Service directory:** `sdk/contentunderstanding/Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding/` **Triggered by:** commit [REDACTED] (PR #58166) by `@chienyuanchang` ## What Changed PR #58166 added: - New `AnalyzeUsageDetails` class exposing billing and token consumption details from completed analyze operations (document page counts, audio/video hours, contextualization tokens, per-model LLM/embedding token dictionary) - New `AnalyzeOperationExtensions.GetUsage()` extension method to retrieve `AnalyzeUsageDetails` from a completed `Operation` - Updated `Sample03_AnalyzeInvoice.md` with a "Get usage details" section and `ContentUnderstandingGetUsage` snippet - Updated `tests/samples/Sample03_AnalyzeInvoice.cs` with the corresponding `#region Snippet:ContentUnderstandingGetUsage` / `#endregion` block The main **README.md** was **not** updated and does not mention the new class or extension method at all. ## Gaps Found - `AnalyzeUsageDetails` is not listed in the **Main classes** section of `README.md` - `AnalyzeOperationExtensions.GetUsage()` is not referenced anywhere in `README.md` - The **Examples** section of `README.md` describes Sample 03 as "Domain-Specific Analysis — Extract structured fields from invoices" but does not mention that Sample 03 now also demonstrates retrieving usage/billing details - No snippet placeholder for `ContentUnderstandingGetUsage` exists in `README.md`, even though the snippet is already defined in the test file and shown in the sample markdown 📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **Edit** `sdk/contentunderstanding/Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding/README.md` **Change 1 — Main classes section:** Add `AnalyzeUsageDetails` to the "Main classes" list. Find the existing block: ```markdown ### Main classes * **`ContentUnderstandingClient`** - The main client for analyzing content, as well as creating, managing, and configuring analyzers * **`AnalysisResult`** - Contains the structured results of an analysis operation, including content elements, markdown, and metadata ``` Replace with: ```markdown ### Main classes * **`ContentUnderstandingClient`** - The main client for analyzing content, as well as creating, managing, and configuring analyzers * **`AnalysisResult`** - Contains the structured results of an analysis operation, including content elements, markdown, and metadata * **`AnalyzeUsageDetails`** - Contains billing and token consumption details from a completed analyze operation. Retrieve via `AnalyzeOperationExtensions.GetUsage()` on a completed `Operation`. ``` **Change 2 — Examples section:** Update the Sample 03 bullet to mention usage/billing details. Find: ```markdown * **Domain-Specific Analysis** - Extract structured fields from invoices using `prebuilt-invoice` ``` Replace with: ```markdown * **Domain-Specific Analysis** - Extract structured fields from invoices using `prebuilt-invoice`, and retrieve usage/billing details (page counts, token consumption) via `AnalyzeOperationExtensions.GetUsage()` ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets The `ContentUnderstandingGetUsage` snippet is already defined in the test file at: `sdk/contentunderstanding/Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding/tests/samples/Sample03_AnalyzeInvoice.cs` The snippet is bounded by `#region Snippet:ContentUnderstandingGetUsage` / `#endregion`. **No new test code is needed.** The only change needed is adding the placeholder to README.md. Add a new subsection in the README under the **Examples** heading (e.g., after the existing "Analyze content" bullet list) or within the "Key concepts" / "Asynchronous operations" section. Insert: ```markdown ### Retrieve usage and billing details After an analyze operation completes, use `GetUsage()` to retrieve token and page consumption: ``` ``` ``` > The `Update-Snippets.ps1` script will populate this placeholder automatically. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README already contains all required sections (Getting started, Key concepts, Examples, Troubleshooting, Next steps, Contributing). No structural changes are needed beyond the targeted additions above. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/contentunderstanding/Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/contentunderstanding/Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 contentunderstanding` — confirm that the `ContentUnderstandingGetUsage` placeholder in README.md is replaced with the code from `Sample03_AnalyzeInvoice.cs` 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 contentunderstanding` — no public API changes are expected, but run to confirm the listing files are up to date 5. `dotnet format sdk/contentunderstanding/Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding/` ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > [!WARNING] > > ⚠️ Firewall blocked 2 domains > > The following domains were blocked by the firewall during workflow execution: > > - `pkgs.dev.azure.com` > - `releaseassets.githubusercontent.com` > > To allow these domains, add them to the `network.allowed` list in your workflow frontmatter: > > ```yaml > network: > allowed: > - defaults > - "pkgs.dev.azure.com" > - "releaseassets.githubusercontent.com" > ``` > > See [Network Configuration](https://github.github.com/gh-aw/reference/network/) for more information. > > > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24705795153/agentic_workflow) · ● 347.9K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58427","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-21T05:36:11Z","updated_at":"2026-04-21T05:36:11Z"}

No structural changes are needed beyond the targeted additions above. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/contentunderstanding/Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/contentunderstanding/Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 contentunderstanding` — confirm that the `ContentUnderstandingGetUsage` placeholder in README.md is replaced with the code from `Sample03_AnalyzeInvoice.cs` 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 contentunderstanding` — no public API changes are expected, but run to confirm the listing files are up to date 5. `dotnet format sdk/contentunderstanding/Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding/` ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > [!WARNING] > > ⚠️ Firewall blocked 2 domains > > The following domains were blocked by the firewall during workflow execution: > > - `pkgs.dev.azure.com` > - `releaseassets.githubusercontent.com` > > To allow these domains, add them to the `network.allowed` list in your workflow frontmatter: > > ```yaml > network: > allowed: > - defaults > - "pkgs.dev.azure.com" > - "releaseassets.githubusercontent.com" > ``` > > See [Network Configuration](https://github.github.com/gh-aw/reference/network/) for more information. > > > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24705795153/agentic_workflow) · ● 347.9K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58427","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-21T05:36:11Z","updated_at":"2026-04-21T05:36:11Z"}

arguments.message: Thank you for your feedback. Tagging and routing to the team member(s) best able to assist.

arguments.body:
## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** This bot-filed issue identifies a documentation gap in the `Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding` README — the new `AnalyzeUsageDetails` class and `AnalyzeOperationExtensions.GetUsage()` extension method added in PR #58166 are not reflected in the package README.

<details>
<summary>📋 Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding`
**Affected API:** `AnalyzeUsageDetails` class, `AnalyzeOperationExtensions.GetUsage()` extension method
**Scenarios:**
- README "Main classes" section does not list `AnalyzeUsageDetails`
- README "Examples" section does not mention usage/billing retrieval in the Sample 03 description
- No `ContentUnderstandingGetUsage` snippet placeholder exists in README (snippet is defined in `Sample03_AnalyzeInvoice.cs`)

**Root ask:** Update `sdk/contentunderstanding/Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding/README.md` to document the new class/method and add a snippet placeholder for `ContentUnderstandingGetUsage`.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🔎 Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>

This is a documentation gap issue, not a code bug. The implementation was added in PR #58166 (commit `[REDACTED]`).

**Suggested investigation steps:**
1. Review PR #58166 to understand the full scope of the `AnalyzeUsageDetails` addition
2. Update `README.md` Main classes section to include `AnalyzeUsageDetails`
3. Update the Sample 03 description in the Examples section to mention usage/billing retrieval
4. Add a `ContentUnderstandingGetUsage` snippet placeholder in README and run `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 contentunderstanding` to populate it
</details>

<details>
<summary>🏷️ Label Confidence</summary>

- **Category:** `Client` — `Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding` is a data plane client library (not management/provisioning)
- **Service:** `Cognitive - Content Understanding` — explicitly named in the issue, matches the service directory `sdk/contentunderstanding/`
- **Confidence:** High — package name and service directory are explicitly stated; no ambiguity between categories or services
</details>

<details>
<summary>👥 Owner Routing</summary>

- **Matched CODEOWNERS entry:** `# ServiceLabel: %Cognitive - Content Understanding` (line 141) — service label matches exactly
- **AzureSdkOwners:** none listed
- **ServiceOwners:** @bojunehsu @changjian-wang @chienyuanchang @yungshinlintw
- **Routing action:** Added "Service Attention" label; no assignment (ServiceOwners path, no AzureSdkOwners); "needs-team-attention" not added (issue is bot-filed, not customer-reported)
- **Scan notes:** Scanned CODEOWNERS bottom-to-top; the `%Cognitive - Content Understanding` entry at line 141 was the first match found
</details>
